decision: Added Hard Rule to CLAUDE.md §Hard-Rules: 'Never ignore a Codex review on a PR'. Every chatgpt-codex-connector inline finding must be (a) fixed via follow-up commit, (b) explicitly rejected with a thread reply, or (c) spun off to a tracked issue — before merge. Triggered by founder noticing Codex's P2 finding on PR #487 (stale ADR-0008→0009 cross-refs) had to be checked manually; that finding had in fact been addressed via commit 76c6657 but the workflow had no codified gate forcing that check.
rationale: Codex reviews are mechanical, fast, and catch real cross-reference / API-shape drift that human reviewers miss when PRs land at 02:00. Silently admin-merging over findings trains the team to stop reading them — exactly the failure mode the 'Verify rule against MERGED tree' rule was meant to prevent for our own rules. Codifying the gate makes 'check Codex before merge' a non-negotiable step like DCO sign-off.

decision: Ratified the RFC-0105 EXCEPTION line (Charter §5.13 / RFC-0090 Three-Surface Rule). Watch is the one capability where CLI and MCP are NOT byte-identical tools because their lifecycles genuinely differ — MCP has long-lived start_watch/stop_watch/watch_status; CLI has foreground mycelium watch that exits on Ctrl-C. Both surfaces drive the same mycelium_core::watch::WatchEngine so reactive behavior (debounce/ignore/re-extract/redb persist) is byte-identical by construction. mycelium watch --status will emit the byte-identical watch_status JSON anchor when the CLI follow-up lands. EXCEPTION codified in skills/index-management/SKILL.md frontmatter comment.
rationale: This was the last open governance item from the v0.1.18 release. The EXCEPTION was always architecturally justified (foreground-vs-server is genuinely surface-shaped, not fakeable parity) and the parity bridge through WatchEngine is verified by the existing tests. Ratifying closes the open ledger item without leaving v0.1.19's first release blocked on a docs gap.

RFC-0114 + Phase 1 core (overnight, autonomous): graph-native project health grade (A-F). TSA inventory #3 (health grading), but RE-GROUNDED — Mycelium has no cyclomatic complexity / coverage (ADR-0010: no LSP, no test runner), so instead of porting TSA's CC/coverage scorer, used Mycelium's graph-native metrics it uniquely has (dead_symbols, isolated_symbols, edge/node density; degree/clustering reserved for Phase 2). Pure scorer core crates/mycelium-core/src/health.rs: HealthGrade{A..F}, HealthMetrics, HealthReport, score() = weighted mean (0.45 dead-code + 0.35 isolation + 0.20 connectivity) → 0-100 → grade bands A>=90.../F<60; empty project fails closed (F/0). 7 TDD tests, fmt+clippy clean (mul_add for the weighted sum). New module + lib.rs decl ONLY — zero collision with #572. Phase 2 (after design review + #572): Store::health() adapter over public API + project-health CLI+MCP (1:1) + Skill (Three-Surface).
rationale: Differentiated angle: a cross-language structural-health grade from the RCIG graph is on-brand (engine value) and something CC-based tools can't do cross-language. Built the testable pure core now (collision-free) while design is reviewed, mirroring RFC-0113's classify.rs pattern. Continuous value per /goal while #572 blocks the classifier integration.

decision: RFC-0113 (design): stdlib/builtin callee classification tier — the #1 TSA-reuse opportunity. After founder pointed at tree-sitter-analyzer ('参考我的tree-sitter-analyzer项目应该可以留用'), the deep-dive ranked this top: TSA's synapse_resolver/_constants.py (_FALLBACK_STDLIB + builtin/external tables) + __init__.py cascade lift callee classification 83.9%→95.9% with ZERO LSP. Surfaced as an additive `class` field on existing get-callees/get-dead-symbols output — NO new tool, Three-Surface clean. ADR-0010 compliant (static tables = the sanctioned no-LSP precision lever; SCIP remains the reserved full-type-precision path).
rationale: Highest precision/effort ratio of the TSA inventory, directly extends just-shipped RFC-0103, zero ADR-0010 risk, non-colliding with the parallel PM (engine domain, not CI/release). decision: Patched both published RFC-0111 SDKs (Node + Python): reject leading-'-' bare positionals (argv smuggling — CLI would re-parse as a flag), and add a 64 MiB streaming output cap to the Python runner mirroring Node's execFile maxBuffer (subprocess.run buffers unbounded). PR #590.
rationale: execFile/subprocess avoid the shell so no injection, but a user query of '--root' still smuggles a flag; '--' separator unusable because our flags follow positionals. Unbounded Python stdout can OOM the host. Both are public, shipped surfaces — warrant a security patch.

decision: RFC-0118 Part A.2: gated all 19 graph-theory queries in store/mod.rs to operate on the real-symbol induced subgraph. Added Store::symbol_universe() as the single source of truth for the node universe (>-qualified symbol nodes filtered by is_real_symbol). Each gated query excludes NodeKind::Unresolved phantoms as nodes AND as edge endpoints; centrality normalization denominators count |real symbols|. All 19 landed GATE-FULL (none GATE-OUTPUT-ONLY or DEFERRED) because the dominant pattern (universe + sym_set/idx-restricted traversal) made the induced subgraph fall out of the universe swap, and the 5 raw-degree queries each had one local degree expression to induce. page_rank refactored to route through symbol_universe() (byte-identical no-op). ADR-0012 documents the decision + per-query disposition. 23 new tests in store/tests.rs, each vs a phantom-free twin oracle. AC-23 added+ticked in RFC-0118.
rationale: Part A gated only the 3 demonstrably-leaking set queries (all_symbols/page_rank/rank_symbols). The centrality/cycle/k-core family still built its universe with the p.contains('>') string heuristic, admitting >-qualified phantoms and counting raw phantom-edge degree — reintroducing the exact resolver noise RFC-0118 set out to remove, on a different surface. Centralizing on symbol_universe() replaces 19 copies of the heuristic with one typed gate; restricting edge traversal to real endpoints makes every metric a property of the real-symbol subgraph. Back-compatible (is_real_symbol is a negative gate; kindless stores unchanged).

decision: RFC-0118 Part B: resolve BARE implicit-self method calls under multi-match by recording a synthetic ReceiverContext{receiver:"self", self_type:Some(enclosing type)} for unresolved bare method calls inside a class/type body; enclosing type found via enclosing_self_type (lexical for Java/C#/C++/Ruby, go_receiver_type for Go). Also fixed Ruby caller attribution to Class>method via a Ruby-scoped class/module walk in enclosing_function_path gated on a method/singleton_method ancestor.
rationale: A bare self-method call with a duplicate method name across types was left as an unresolved stub, so get-callers returned a confident-but-wrong empty set. The plumbing (infer_receiver_type rule a + post-merge resolve_call_site_contexts) already existed; only the extraction-time capture was missing for receiver==None.

decision: RFC-0118 Part B: Ruby bare implicit-self calls WITHOUT parentheses (`speak`) are NOT resolved; only the parenthesised `speak()` form is. Core-only fix; no Ruby pack change.
rationale: In tree-sitter-ruby a bare `speak` parses as (identifier), grammatically indistinguishable from a local-variable read. Emitting @reference.call for every bare identifier would manufacture phantom call edges for every variable reference, violating conservatism. The parenthesised form parses as a (call) node and is unambiguous.

decision: Restrict bare implicit-self method resolution to Java/C#/C++/Ruby via IMPLICIT_SELF_SCOPES (method-kind, container-kind) pair allow-list; exclude Python/Rust/TS/JS/Go
rationale: Codex P2 on PR #680: enclosing_class_chain fired for ALL type-container languages, but only implicit-this languages treat a bare speak() as self.speak(). Binding it elsewhere manufactures false caller edges (e.g. TS A>run->A>speak). Node kinds collide (class_declaration=Java/C#+TS/JS; function_definition=C++/Python; method_declaration=Java/C#+Go) so the (method,container) PAIR is the only reliable structural discriminator. Added 3 RED-first exclusion tests (py/rust/ts).

decision: serve --mcp loads the mtime-newer of index.redb/index.rmp (was: redb-if-exists, no freshness check) and resolves allowed-roots to [--root] when --allowed-roots omitted (was: [CWD], which overrode --root)
rationale: mycelium index rewrites only index.rmp; the stale leftover index.redb shadowed it so MCP served wrong/empty answers vs CLI (get-callers Store>upsert_node: MCP stale 429 vs CLI fresh 430). Separately, defaulting allowed-roots to CWD made mycelium_subscribe fail with root_not_allowed whenever serve launched from outside --root. pick_index_path tie->redb + load-time re-persist keeps the two formats convergent without regressing RFC-0107 reactive watch.

decision: Generalize the kind-aware call guard from 'is TypeAlias' to 'is non-callable kind' via new helper Store::is_callable_target_kind; blocked set = {Module, File, Import, Export, TypeAlias}. Applied in all three resolution passes (resolve_bare_call_stubs_simple, resolve_import_aware_stubs, resolve_call_site_contexts). .go> exception stays scoped to TypeAlias only. Interface/Enum left callable conservatively (no observed collision).
rationale: page-rank --edge-kind calls ranked crates/mycelium-mcp/src/lib.rs>push (a `mod push;` NodeKind::Module) #1 at score 0.031 with 60 false callers: every stdlib Vec::push/String::push .push() call collapsed onto the same-named Module by bare-name resolution. Same bug class as #682 (call->TypeAlias) but target is Module. A function call can never resolve to a module/file/import/export. Kept Struct/Class/EnumMember (ctors) and Field/Property/Variable/Constant/Parameter (closure pointers) callable to avoid dropping real edges. After fix, lib.rs>push gone from top 200 page-rank; get-callers returns only the 3 real push:: module refs, not the 60 .push() sites.

decision: Hyphae: add Evaluator::eval_checked + EvalError to turn the silent-empty trap (unsupported attribute name like [lang=] or unsupported pseudo like :frobnicate(), both hitting the evaluator's `_ => Vec::new()` fallthrough) into an explicit, actionable error; kept bare `eval` unchanged and routed only the two user-facing surfaces (CLI query.rs::execute + MCP mycelium_query) through eval_checked for Three-Surface parity
rationale: An empty match set is indistinguishable to an AI agent from 'nothing matches' — the worst failure mode. Validation is a pure AST walk, so a separate eval_checked avoids rippling a Result signature across 6 callers (the :context/routing paths intentionally still use bare eval + parse-fallback). Investigation found the prompt's other premises (descendant combinator and [language=]/[file=] failing silently) were stale: those are fully implemented on develop. No grammar/RFC change needed — this is a bug-fix-only guard, NOT new selectors.

decision: Fix post-budget count drift in apply_budget (general rule, not per-call-site fixup): when exactly one array is truncated and the object has a total_count sibling and count == pre-truncation array length, rewrite count to the post-truncation length
rationale: count semantics split by payload family: paginated payloads (entry_points, all_symbols) carry count=page-size + total_count=full-total, so count must follow the budget-truncated array; total-only payloads (dead_symbols, isolated_symbols, reachable) document count as the full pre-budget total and have no total_count — the total_count-sibling guard preserves them. One change in core fixes both tools on both surfaces, keeping CLI<->MCP byte-identical by construction (Charter 5.13). Surgical post-budget fixups at 4 call sites were rejected as drift-prone.

decision: RFC-0123 drafted: consolidate the 95-tool MCP surface into 11 action facades (query/context standalone + symbols/callgraph/reach/hierarchy/graph/rank/analyze/admin/subscribe with required action enums); responses stay byte-identical, only the tool surface changes. Proposes amending RFC-0090/Charter §5.13 from tool-level to action-level CLI↔MCP 1:1 (action name = legacy tool name minus mycelium_ prefix, mechanically derivable from the CLI subcommand). Migration: --tool-surface=facade|legacy|both, default both→facade over 2 minors, legacy removed at next major.
rationale: Measured live on develop@ce85709: tools/list = 189,624 bytes ≈ 47.4K tokens for 95 tools (avg 1,996 B/tool), paid per-request by schema-injecting clients; agent-experience QA found tool-selection paralysis among 5 overlapping who-calls-X tools; industry threshold ~40 tools; siblings (tree-sitter-analyzer 8 facades, codegraph ~10) already consolidated. Prototype callgraph facade schema measured at 2,721 B for 10 actions → estimated post-consolidation surface ≈35.6 KB (≈81% reduction, gate set at 80%/37,925 B). SDK/IDE untouched (they wrap the CLI per RFC-0111). Governance change flagged for founder ratification via companion ADR; implementation is phased follow-up PRs, not this RFC.

decision: Callee tree (CLI get-callee-tree + MCP mycelium_get_callee_tree) no longer emits unresolved callees as placeholder leaves; Store::callee_tree gates children on is_real_symbol(id) && path_of(id).is_some() and collapses the rest into a new CalleeNode.unresolved_callees count, serialized as "unresolved_callees": N per node (omitted when 0). Caller tree verified unaffected (phantoms are never call sources; 614-node dogfood caller tree had 0 stubs) and left unchanged. ADR-0013 written (extends ADR-0012's real-symbol principle to the tree payload); CHANGELOG Unreleased/Changed entry; skills/call-graph/SKILL.md shape doc updated. TDD RED-first: store_callee_tree_collapses_unresolved_stub_callees + store_callee_tree_counts_dangling_edge_targets_as_unresolved + store_callee_tree_kindless_store_children_unchanged (core), get_callee_tree_collapses_unresolved_into_count (mcp + cli twins).
rationale: Dogfood measurement: index.rs>index_path depth-3 tree was 142/173 (82%) content-free stub leaves (reported as path:<unknown> on stale snapshots, bare stub names on fresh ones) — pure token noise for agents. Collapsing preserves the information (count survives, Σ=142) while shrinking the payload 173→31 nodes (7251→2976 bytes). Gating in core keeps CLI↔MCP byte-identical by construction (Charter §5.13); is_real_symbol is a negative gate so kind-less programmatic stores are back-compatible.

rationale: Live QA: get_source_span Store>upsert_node returned 447-4989 (~183KB impl block) for a 3-line fn; main.rs>main returned 1-2077 (whole file). mycelium_context shipped these in code_blocks, breaking token-dense source reading. After fix: 648-650 / 1163-1180. Spans come from the @definition anchor; paths come from @name (+container anchor for methods), so re-anchoring non-method captures changes spans only — all 866 core tests green.

decision: Thread the RFC-0102 OutputBudget through the three remaining unbudgeted tool families on both surfaces: (1) query/mycelium_query — matches capped at max_nodes via the existing apply_budget key list (added matches), payload gains total_count so count follows the returned page (the #746 rule); CLI --format=json now emits the shared { matches, count, total_count } object (was a bare string array). (2) get_cross_refs — importers/extended_by/implemented_by added to the edge-key list so every group is capped at max_edges (only callers happened to match before). (3) get_callee_tree/get_caller_tree — new apply_tree_budget in mycelium-core::budget walks the { root } payload BREADTH-FIRST and keeps the first max_nodes nodes; each node with cut direct children carries children_truncated:K (ADR-0013 unresolved_callees style); root gains the standard truncated/total_available/budget{} metadata via a write_budget_metadata helper shared with apply_budget so the shapes are byte-identical. Tree serialization moved to mycelium_core::queries::{callee,caller}_tree_payload so both surfaces share one builder. Default text-mode CLI output is never silently truncated; budgeting applies in JSON mode or when --budget is explicit, with a stderr truncation footer for explicit text-mode budgets.
rationale: Live QA: default mycelium_get_callee_tree dumped ~373KB (~90K tokens pre-ADR-0013) and mycelium_query returned 39.5KB from one .method selector with no way to cap either, while get_callers politely truncates — inconsistent budget coverage trains agents to avoid the un-budgeted tools. BFS (not DFS) was chosen for the tree cap because a budgeted tree is an overview: BFS keeps every direct callee/caller before any grandchild, whereas DFS keeps one leftmost spine at full depth and reads as <symbol only calls one thing>. Per-node children_truncated keeps the cut honest at the exact node where it happened. Shared core builders keep CLI<->MCP byte-identical by construction (Charter 5.13).

decision: RFC-0124: Hyphae simple-selector grammar relaxed from `base attribute* pseudo*` to `base (attribute | pseudo)*` — attribute filters and pseudo-classes accepted in any order. Parser-only change: single loop dispatches on LBracket/Colon into the same two AST Vecs; AST and evaluator untouched. Normative semantics: filters compose by set intersection, so order carries no meaning (a[x]:p ≡ a:p[x]); structural pseudos (:first-child/:nth-child/...) already rank against ALL store siblings, never the filtered candidate set, so they commute too — CSS semantics, pinned by tests rather than re-implemented. eval_checked validation covers the new positions for free (operates on AST). CLI query help + MCP mycelium_query description gained the lockstep example `*:calls(#Foo)[file=src/x.rs]`; documented_examples_parse.rs guards it plus the skills/hyphae-query composition example `.class:has(.method:calls(#log))[language=python]`, which had been advertised since RFC-0091 but was unparseable until this change.
rationale: Live agent QA: `*:calls(#upsert_node)[file=...]` — the natural filter-callers-by-file query — hard-errored UnexpectedToken(LBracket) because RFC-0091 pinned attributes before pseudos, an ordering CSS does not impose. Strictly additive superset; sanity on real index: 476 unfiltered callers → 15 with [file=crates/mycelium-core/src/queries.rs], both orderings byte-identical.
